data.title: The Houthi rebels stated they would join the fight if the enemy returned to the battlefield.
data.description: On the 21st local time, the Houthi leader stated, "If the enemy returns to the battlefield, we will join the fight." The Houthi leader said, "Our position is clear and open: we will never remain neutral in the face of the aggression of the United States and Israel against the Islamic Republic of Iran."

data.title: Houthi Leader Vows to Join Battle if Hostilities Resume
data.description: On April 22, local time, Houthi leader declared readiness to engage in combat if adversaries return to the battlefield. According to BlockBeats, the leader emphasized their clear and public stance against aggression by the United States and Israel towards the Islamic Republic of Iran, stating they will not remain neutral.

data.title: White House official: US Vice President Vance has not yet departed for Iran for talks.
data.description: White House official: US Vice President Vance has not yet departed for Iran for talks. (Jinshi)

data.title: Italy's Deficit-to-GDP Ratio Expected to Align with Previous Targets
data.description: Italy is projected to maintain its deficit-to-GDP ratio at approximately 2.8% for this year and 2.6% by 2027, according to sources. This forecast aligns closely with the targets set in September of the previous year. According to Jin10, these figures suggest that Italy is on track to meet its fiscal objectives, reflecting a stable economic outlook.

data.title: U.S. Senator Questions Digital Assets' Role in Financial System
data.description:
U.S. Senator Cynthia Lummis raised a question during a hearing on April 22 regarding the inclusion of digital assets in the U.S. financial system. According to BlockBeats, she asked Federal Reserve Chair nominee, Walsh, whether digital assets should be integrated to provide Americans with new investment opportunities and consumer protection.

Walsh responded affirmatively, stating that digital assets are already a part of the structure of the U.S. financial services system.

data.title: Iran fires on oil tanker victim of crypto fraud
data.description: Iran recently announced a $1 per barrel Bitcoin toll for oil tankers passing through the Strait of Hormuz. Scammers immediately impersonated Iranian government officials and sent false messages to ship owners. On April 18, at least one oil tanker that had already paid the scammers in Bitcoin was intercepted by Iranian vessels and forced to turn back while attempting to pass through the strait. Greek maritime risk management company MARISKS warned that criminals are demanding payment from ship owners in Bitcoin or Tether, instructing them to submit documents for evaluation by purported Iranian security services. MARISKS confirmed that these requests are scams, and the identities of the affected companies are currently unknown.

data.title: Federal Reserve Nominee Criticizes Balance Sheet's Role
data.description: Federal Reserve nominee Waller has expressed concerns about the central bank's balance sheet, stating that it has negatively impacted the achievement of its dual mandate. According to ChainCatcher, Waller's remarks highlight ongoing debates about the effectiveness of the Federal Reserve's current financial strategies.

data.title: Federal Reserve Nominee Walsh Comments on Economic Improvement
data.description: Federal Reserve nominee Walsh has stated that the economy is showing signs of improvement and has the potential for further enhancement. According to ChainCatcher, Walsh emphasized the strengthening economic potential during his remarks.

data.title: U.S. Lawmakers Introduce PACE Act to Modernize Payment Systems
data.description: U.S. lawmakers have introduced the Payment and Settlement System Act (PACE Act) to modernize the country's payment systems. According to BlockBeats, the legislation aims to allow eligible companies direct access to the Federal Reserve's payment system, which is expected to reduce delays, lower costs, and expedite fund transfers for consumers and businesses. The proposal has garnered support from fintech and cryptocurrency communities, aiming to make payments faster, cheaper, and more competitive.

data.title: Federal Reserve Nominee Collaborates on Balance Sheet Reduction
data.description: Federal Reserve nominee Waller has announced collaboration with the Treasury Secretary to advance the reduction of the Federal Reserve's balance sheet. According to ChainCatcher, this initiative aims to streamline the financial operations of the central bank. The move is part of broader efforts to manage economic stability and monetary policy.

data.title: Fed Chair Nominee Waller: Interest Rates Should Rely on High-Quality, Forward-Looking Economic Data
data.description: Fed Chair nominee Christopher Waller emphasized the importance of basing interest rate decisions on high-quality, forward-looking economic data. According to Jin10, Waller highlighted the need for more discussions within the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) to ensure that monetary policy decisions are well-informed and effective. He stressed that a comprehensive understanding of economic indicators is crucial for setting appropriate interest rates that align with the current economic landscape.

data.title: Admiral Paparo Highlights Bitcoin's Role in U.S. Security Strategy
data.description: Admiral Samuel Paparo addressed the Senate Armed Services Committee, emphasizing Bitcoin's significance as a computer science tool for U.S. power projection and security. According to NS3.AI, Paparo described Bitcoin as a peer-to-peer, zero-trust transfer of value, noting that INDOPACOM is examining its cryptography, blockchain, and proof-of-work architecture for potential cyber applications. While Paparo refrained from offering legislative recommendations during the open session, he indicated plans to provide more detailed insights in writing and within a classified setting.

data.title: Crypto Hackers Rush to Launder Stolen Funds Amid Industry Efforts to Block Them
data.description: Hackers responsible for the largest cryptocurrency theft of the year are swiftly moving to launder their stolen assets. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the industry's urgent response to prevent the laundering process. The crypto community is actively working to block these efforts, employing various strategies to track and freeze the stolen funds. Despite these measures, the hackers continue to find ways to circumvent security protocols, posing a significant challenge to the industry. The incident underscores the ongoing vulnerabilities within the crypto sector and the need for enhanced security measures to protect digital assets.

data.title: U.S. Negotiators Arrive in Pakistan for Talks
data.description: U.S. negotiators have arrived in Pakistan over the past two days for discussions, according to BlockBeats. This development was reported by Xinhua News Agency, citing Pakistani sources. The arrival of the American delegation marks a significant step in ongoing diplomatic engagements between the two countries. Further details on the agenda or outcomes of these talks have not been disclosed.

data.title: WTI crude oil rose nearly 4% intraday, while Brent crude oil rose 3.65% intraday.
data.description: According to Gate data, both WTI and Brent crude oil prices continued to rise, with WTI crude oil reaching $92 per barrel, up nearly 4% on the day. Brent crude oil rose 3.65% to $93.68 per barrel.

data.title: Federal Reserve Nominee Kevin Warsh Comments on Inflation Trends
data.description: Federal Reserve Chairman nominee Kevin Warsh has expressed optimism regarding the current inflation trends. According to NS3.AI, Warsh noted that the trajectory of inflation is showing positive signs, suggesting a favorable economic outlook. His comments come amid ongoing discussions about monetary policy and its impact on the economy.

data.title: ElizaOS Faces Class-Action Lawsuit Over Alleged False Advertising and Market Manipulation
data.description: ElizaOS, formerly known as AI16Z, is currently embroiled in a class-action lawsuit concerning accusations of false advertising and market manipulation. According to NS3.AI, the plaintiffs allege that the developers of ElizaOS misappropriated the branding of Andreessen Horowitz's a16z and falsely marketed their manually operated system as an AI agent with independent investment capabilities. The lawsuit claims that these actions have misled investors and manipulated market perceptions, prompting legal action against the company.

data.title: U.S. Vice President's Trip to Pakistan Remains Uncertain Amid Policy Discussions
data.description:
U.S. Vice President Vance's departure from Washington to Pakistan is currently uncertain, according to BlockBeats. CNN reports that the White House plans to hold further meetings on Tuesday to discuss subsequent matters, with Vice President Vance participating.

A White House official stated, "The White House is holding more policy meetings, and the Vice President will attend these meetings." Vance was originally scheduled to depart for Islamabad on Tuesday morning. However, a crucial issue remains unresolved: whether the Iranian delegation will accompany him.

As of late Tuesday morning local time, Iran has not yet decided whether to send officials to Pakistan for the second round of talks.

data.title: Fed Chair Nominee Wash: Trump Has Not Asked for Pre-Determined Rate Cuts
data.description: Fed Chair nominee Wash stated that U.S. President Donald Trump has never requested him to pre-determine interest rates, and he would not agree to such a request. According to Jin10, Wash emphasized his commitment to maintaining independence in monetary policy decisions during his nomination hearing.

data.title: Cryptocurrency Political Groups Amass $180 Million for Midterm Elections
data.description: Political groups associated with the cryptocurrency industry have accumulated a substantial $180 million fund in preparation for this year's midterm elections. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the potential for these groups to outspend even some of the Republican Party's most notable super PACs. This financial power underscores the growing influence of the cryptocurrency sector in political arenas, as these groups aim to sway policy and regulatory decisions in their favor. The amassed funds reflect the industry's strategic efforts to secure favorable outcomes in the upcoming elections, potentially impacting legislative directions related to digital currencies.
